lessons.title: I Have a Pet
lessons.topic: Cat, Dog, Bird and the I like pattern

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which pet has a long, furry tail and likes to chase mice?
questions.hint: This pet often purrs when happy.

questions.question_text: Which pet loves to fetch a ball?
questions.hint: This pet is known as man's best friend.

questions.question_text: Which pet can sing beautifully and fly high?
questions.hint: This pet has wings and loves to perch.

steps.title: Tom's Pets
steps.text: Tom has a pet. It is a cat. Tom says, 'I like my cat.' The cat is with a bird. Do you like birds?

questions.question: What does Tom say about his cat?

steps.explanation: Say 'I like' to talk about pets you love.

steps.audio_text: I have a parrot. She loves to sing and fly around. Do you like birds?

questions.question: What does her parrot love to do?

questions.question_text: 'I like ___.' Which animal fits?
Which word is a pet? (Vocabulary question)
What pet can fly and sing?
True or False: 'I like birds.' means I dislike them.

lessons.title: I Don't Like...
lessons.topic: Rabbit, Mouse, Turtle and negative preference

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which pet is small, fast, and often has whiskers?
questions.hint: This pet likes to run in a wheel!

questions.question_text: Which pet can fly and loves to talk?
Which animal slides on the ground and can be in a garden?
questions.hint: It leaves a slimy trail behind!

steps.title: Turtle's Friends
steps.text: Look! A turtle is slow. I don't like the mouse. I like the rabbit. The rabbit jumps high.

steps.explanation: Use 'I like' for things you enjoy.

steps.audio_text: I don't like mice, but I think turtles are awesome. My brother likes both turtles and rabbits! Do you have a pet turtle?

questions.question: What animals does she think are awesome?

questions.question_text: Which animal has long ears and loves to hop? (Vocabulary question)
I don't like ___. (Grammar question)
True or False: A turtle can fly. (Reading comprehension question)
What is the main topic of the lesson? (Reading comprehension question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which pet likes to swim in a bowl?
questions.hint: This pet has fins and swims around.

questions.question_text: Who has long ears and hops around?
questions.hint: It loves to nibble on carrots.

questions.question_text: Which animal barks when it sees someone?
questions.hint: It wags its tail when it's happy.

steps.title: Splash the Fish
steps.text: Do you like water? Splash the fish does! Splash is in the pond. I like Splash because it is cute. Do you like Splash?

steps.explanation: Use this to say what you enjoy.

steps.audio_text: Do you like fish? They are colorful and swim in the water. Fish are so cute to watch!

questions.question_text: Do you like ___ in the water? (Grammar question)
Which word means 'pretty and small'? (Vocabulary question)
Where do fish live? (Reading comprehension question)
True or False: Fish can walk on land.
What can you say if you like something? (Grammar question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which pet likes to climb trees and chase mice?
questions.hint: This pet says 'meow' and is very curious!

questions.question_text: Which pet is known for swimming in a bowl or tank?
questions.hint: This pet has fins and likes water!

questions.question_text: Which pet is small, furry, and spins in a wheel?
questions.hint: This pet loves to nibble on seeds and fruits!

steps.title: Pets and Preferences
steps.text: My name is Mia. I like dogs and fish. I have one big dog. I don't like snakes. Do you like fish? Yes, I do!

questions.question: Does Mia have a big dog?

steps.audio_text: I have a cute little rabbit named Fluffy. Fluffy likes to hop around and eat carrots. I enjoy watching Fluffy play in the garden.

questions.question: What is the name of the rabbit?
Does Fluffy like to hop around?

steps.prompt: Tell me one pet you like and one pet you don't like. You can say: I like cats, I don't like frogs.
steps.expected_text: i like cats i don't like frogs

questions.question_text: I ___ cats. (Grammar question)
Which animal is known for having whiskers? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: A hamster is bigger than a rabbit.
Do you like to play with dogs? (Reading comprehension question)
